items.title: Add a worked example to the convolution lab
items.body: I'm finding the convolution lab hard to follow without a worked example. A toy x[n] = {1, 1, 0, 1} convolved with h[n] = {1, -1} stepped out frame-by-frame would help anchor the procedure.
items.author: Owner-Fixture

replies.body: Agreed — the visual aid jumps from a sketch straight to the result without a step-by-step pass.
replies.author: Alpha

replies.body: Could we add a slider that scrubs through each n step alongside the running sum?
replies.author: Bravo

replies.body: Replying to Alpha — yes a stepped pass would unblock me on §3.8-1, the first time I hit the lab.
replies.author: Charlie
replies.replyToBody: Agreed — the visual aid jumps from a sketch straight to the result without a step-by-step pass.

replies.body: Same blocker on §3.8-3. A worked example would generalize across the lab.
replies.author: Delta

replies.body: +1 for the slider — and please show the running sum alongside the flip-and-shift overlay.
replies.author: Echo
